title: The Hidden Plumbing of Commodity Finance | Odd Lots
title_cn: 商品金融的隐藏管道|零股
summary: We talk about the commodity supply chain all the time. We talk about the ports and the trucks and the ships and all of that. But there's another dimension to moving commodities all around the world, which is actually paying for it. Who funds the oil tanker and what happens when that tanker is, say, stuck in the Strait of Hormuz? Commodity finance underpins production, transportation and storage of a wide variety of the things that make the modern world, but you tend to only hear about it when things go wrong. Today we speak with Lewis Hart, head of corporate advisory and banking at Brown Brothers Harriman. We discuss how the business of commodity finance actually works, how risk is priced, what makes for a good or bad warehouse, and the difference between financing a commodity you can hedge (like oil) versus one where there's no futures market (like cashews). (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Africa’s Population Boom Could Power Its Next Growth Era
title_cn: 为什么非洲人口激增可以推动其下一个增长时代
summary: Could Africa’s long-misunderstood population boom become its greatest economic advantage? Economist and author Joe Studwell joins host Merryn Somerset Webb to discuss his book, How Africa Works: Success and Failure on the World's Last Developmental Frontier. He argues that rising population density is already transforming the continent by creating deeper markets, boosting agricultural productivity, supporting urbanization and making manufacturing more viable. While governance, debt and commodity dependence remain risks, he sees a more diverse, locally driven growth story emerging across Africa. (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: The dominance of a few technology giants in the benchmark S&P 500 Index is now carrying over into the niche market for dividend futures and options.

title: How a Nigerian Startup Became an Fintech Unicorn
title_cn: 尼日利亚初创公司如何成为金融科技独角兽
summary: African fintech unicorn Moniepoint was founded in 2015 and has grown into one of the continent's standout success stories. From powering payments for small businesses to building critical financial infrastructure, the company has scaled rapidly and earned its place among Africa's most valuable tech firms. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja spoke to Felix Ike, the company's co-founder and CTO, about Moniepoint's journey from startup to unicorn, and what it takes to build at scale in Africa's fast-evolving tech landscape.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX, OpenAI Windfall Fuels Bets on Next-Wave Asian AI Winners
title_cn: SpaceX 和 OpenAI 意外之财推动了对下一波亚洲人工智能获胜者的押注
summary: The hunt is on for companies that could benefit from the tailwinds of an unprecedented wave of stock offerings in the US, and investors are increasingly honing in on the Asian supply chain.

title: Philippines’ VP Duterte Asks Senate to Dismiss Impeachment Case
title_cn: 菲律宾副总统杜特尔特要求参议院驳回弹劾案
summary: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte has asked the Senate to throw out the impeachment case against her ahead of the trial, in a bid to thwart a second attempt by opponents to ban her from politics.

title: Spain Vies to Place European To Lead UN Food Agency After 50 Years
title_cn: 西班牙力争让欧洲人领导联合国粮食机构 50 年后
summary: Spain is seeking to place a European at the top of the world’s largest food agency for the first time in more than five decades.

title: Colombian Voters Cast Old Order Into the Abyss
title_cn: 哥伦比亚选民将旧秩序抛入深渊
summary: The presidential election first round threw up an unexpected result that sets up a tense runoff.

title: Trump Says Deal Will ‘Work Out Well’ Even as US, Iran Clash
title_cn: 特朗普表示，即使美国与伊朗发生冲突，协议也会“顺利进行”
summary: US President Donald Trump said talks with Iran over an interim peace deal will “work out well,” even as the countries’ forces clashed again near the Strait of Hormuz.

title: India, Australia Pledge to Boost Undersea Monitoring Efforts
title_cn: 印度和澳大利亚承诺加强海底监测工作
summary: India and Australia agreed to deepen cooperation on maritime surveillance and undersea monitoring, days after the Quad nations unveiled a new initiative aimed at boosting security across the Indo-Pacific.

title: Philippine Senator Accused of Plunder Over Graft Mess Surrenders
title_cn: 菲律宾参议员被指控因贪腐乱局而掠夺投降
summary: Philippine Senator Jinggoy Estrada has turned himself in to police after a court ordered his arrest on corruption charges as political tensions in the Southeast Asian nation escalate.

title: Hormuz Closure Squeezes Petrochemical Markets
title_cn: 霍尔木兹关闭挤压石化市场
summary: Beyond disrupting oil flows, the Iran war is also squeezing the chemical feedstocks that make everyday plastics. BloombergNEF's Philip Geurts speaks to Bloomberg's Lizzy Burden about what the closure of the Strait of Hormuz means for petrochemical markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SoftBank Announces €75 Bln AI Data Center Plan in France
title_cn: 软银宣布在法国投资 750 亿欧元的人工智能数据中心计划
summary: SoftBank plans to invest as much as €75 billion ($87 billion) to build 5 gigawatts of artificial intelligence data center capacity in France, saying the country is poised to become a top European hub for AI infrastructure. SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son met French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris today to discuss the plan. Bloomberg's Peter Elstrom and Caroline Connan report. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Revolving-Door Politics Erode Peru’s Mining Appeal Ahead of Vote
title_cn: 旋转门政治削弱了秘鲁在投票前的矿业吸引力
summary: Rich deposits and low costs have made Peru a mining superpower. But as Peruvians prepare to choose their ninth president in a decade, the industry is losing competitiveness just as demand for critical minerals accelerates.

title: Starmer Braces for Release of Hundreds of Mandelson Messages
title_cn: 斯塔默准备发布数百条曼德尔森信息
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer will be forced to publish hundreds of private exchanges between the UK’s disgraced former ambassador, Peter Mandelson, and members of his government, disclosures that are likely to put his beleaguered premiership in further danger.

title: Hungary Seeks to Oust President in Purge of Orban Allies
title_cn: 匈牙利寻求推翻总统以清洗欧尔班盟友
summary: Hungary plans to amend the constitution to oust the country’s president as Prime Minister Peter Magyar presses ahead with a plan to dismantle Viktor Orban’s regime.

title: Macron Says France Got €93 Billion of Foreign Investment Pledges
title_cn: 马克龙表示法国已获得 930 亿欧元的外国投资承诺
summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced €93 billion ($108 billion) in foreign investments at the “Choose France” summit, with the bulk coming from an investment pledge from SoftBank Group Corp.

title: Vietnam, Philippines Upgrade Ties, Plan More Military Activities
title_cn: 越南与菲律宾升级关系并计划开展更多军事活动
summary: Vietnam and the Philippines are eyeing more joint military activities after their leaders elevated ties to an enhanced strategic partnership in the face of Beijing’s assertiveness in the South China Sea.

title: China Rips New York Times Over Taiwan After Reporter Expelled
title_cn: 记者被驱逐后，中国就台湾问题抨击《纽约时报》
summary: China took aim at the New York Times for its reporting on Taiwan, escalating a dispute that led to the expulsion of a journalist from the world’s second-biggest economy.

title: Ethiopians Vote in Race Incumbent Leader Is Expected to Win
title_cn: 埃塞俄比亚人投票选举现任领导人有望获胜
summary: Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed looks set to secure another five-year term in Monday’s elections, extending a tenure initially marked by sweeping reforms and later marred by civil conflict and rising regional tensions.

title: Russia Risks Fuel Crunch After Record Month of Ukrainian Attacks
title_cn: 乌克兰一个月创纪录袭击事件后，俄罗斯面临燃料短缺的风险
summary: Smoke rises above buildings following a recent drone attack on the Tuapse oil refinery in Tuapse, Krasnodar region on April 29, 2026, amid the ongoing Russian-Ukrainian conflict. (Photo by AFP via Getty Images)

title: Iran War: Ceasefire Deal in Doubt as Israel Steps up Lebanon Offensive | Daybreak Europe 06/01/2026
title_cn: 伊朗战争：以色列加强黎巴嫩攻势，停火协议成疑欧洲黎明 06/01/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

Stocks rose to a record as investors doubled down on the artificial intelligence trade that’s powered equities to all-time highs, even as oil climbed as a US-Iran ceasefire deal remained elusive.

The US and Iran traded messages over the weekend seeking changes to a draft agreement that would extend a ceasefire and open the Strait of Hormuz, but it was unclear whether the sides were making much progress. 

And Nvidia announced it is entering the PC market with a new chip aimed at loosening the stranglehold of Intel technology in that arena and modernizing the machines for the AI era. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US, Iran Keep Hammering at Deal as Strikes Continue
title_cn: 袭击仍在继续，美国和伊朗继续敲定达成协议
summary: The US and Iran are continuing to negotiate a draft agreement aimed at extending a ceasefire and reopening the Strait of Hormuz, even as military strikes continue in parts of the Middle East. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Sends Patrol East of Taiwan After Tokyo-Manila Talks
title_cn: 东京-马尼拉会谈后中国向台湾东部派遣巡逻队
summary: China said it sent Coast Guard vessels on “law enforcement patrols” to the east of Taiwan, a show of displeasure after Japan and the Philippines agreed to hold talks on maritime borders in the area.

title: Sheinbaum Accuses US of Interfering in Mexico’s Politics
title_cn: 希因鲍姆指责美国干涉墨西哥政治
summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum launched her strongest criticism to date against what she says are blunt US attempts to interfere in Mexican domestic politics.

title: Taiwan No. 2 to Visit Palau, Countering China’s Isolation Bid
title_cn: 台湾第二号将访问帕劳，对抗中国的孤立举动
summary: Taiwanese Vice President Hsiao Bi-khim is making her first overseas trip to a diplomatic ally as the democracy’s No. 2 official, countering China’s isolation campaign.

title: US, Iran Trade Deal Drafts as Israel Expands Lebanon Assault
title_cn: 以色列扩大对黎巴嫩的袭击，美国与伊朗草拟贸易协议
summary: The US and Iran traded messages over the weekend seeking changes to a draft agreement that would extend a ceasefire and open the Strait of Hormuz, but it was unclear whether the sides were making much progress.

title: India’s Modi Hosts Sanctioned Myanmar Leader to Build Relations
title_cn: 印度莫迪接待受制裁的缅甸领导人以建立关系
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is hosting Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing’s first foreign trip since the former junta chief became president following a widely-criticized election.

title: Pro-Trump Outsider Wins Colombia Vote, Setting Up Heated Runoff
title_cn: 支持特朗普的局外人赢得哥伦比亚投票，掀起激烈决选
summary: Celebrity lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella unexpectedly topped the first round of Colombia’s presidential vote, as Latin America heads into another highly polarized election.

title: Australia Populist Party Surges to Poll Lead as Budget Struggles
title_cn: 预算困难，澳大利亚民粹党在民意调查中大幅领先
summary: Australia’s populist One Nation party surged past the ruling Labor party to lead a nationwide opinion poll for the first time, highlighting voter disappointment with last month’s budget and reinforcing signs of a fracturing of the conservative side of politics.

title: Powell Says Fed Credibility Lost If President Can Fire Officials
title_cn: 鲍威尔表示，如果总统可以解雇官员，美联储的信誉就会丧失
summary: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said the US central bank would lose the credibility that’s required to support a strong and stable economy if any president were free to dismiss Fed officials over policy disagreements.

title: US, Iran Trade Drafts of Deal
title_cn: 美国与伊朗贸易协议草案
summary: The US and Iran traded messages over the weekend seeking changes to a draft agreement that would extend a ceasefire and open the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hegseth Seeks to Convince Allies Silence Is Best on Taiwan
title_cn: 赫格斯试图说服盟友对台湾保持沉默是最好的
summary: At a security forum in Singapore, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth sought to convince US allies that staying quiet on Taiwan is the best way to project American strength. While time will tell if that’s the case, for the moment it’s a win for Chinese President Xi Jinping.

title: Burnham Opens Door to UK General Election If He Becomes Premier
title_cn: 如果伯纳姆成为首相，他将为英国大选敞开大门
summary: Andy Burnham, the Greater Manchester mayor who wants to replace Keir Starmer as UK prime minister this summer, has left open the possibility that he could call an early general election if he wins power.
